text: Syntax @ for (statement 1; statement 2; statement 3) { // code block to be executed e i=c ; Initialization Statement 1 is executed (one time) before the execution of the code block. False @ Statement 2 defines the condition for executing the code block. Statement 3 is executed (every time) after the code block has been executed. Code Inside Loop Example @ System.out.println(i); I intln(h); ) — Note: Again, you don’'t need curly braces { } for your for-statement if there is only ONE statement in it
